VizChitra Community
Yesterday
~Prakriti
: 
How do you pick 100 stories from a country that could tell a thousand? At VizChitra 2026, Rohit Saran, Managing Editor at The Times of India, will reflect on the making of 100 Ways to See India, shaped by years of working across India’s vast and uneven data landscape. The talk brings out the process of turning India’s complexity into visual narratives, where some numbers speak to the moment and others point to longer patterns. More details about the talk here: https://vizchitra.com/2026/sessions/timely-timeless-stories tickets.vizchitra.com

Curious Photons - DeepTech Community
Friday
Meet n Greet/Ask Help/Events
~Kartikay Bagla
: TLDR: Just DSA questions and basic system design is not enough to clear interviews. Some linux knowledge, networking knowledge, real world infra knowledge, code hygiene, debugging, reading code - these are skills that give you the edge in your interviews. If you're a student, reach out to discuss gaps in your knowledge. If you're a senior engineer or manager, reach out to discuss common pitfalls in your interviewees. — I've been interviewing a lot of engineering candidates lately, and the same issue keeps turning up. They can parrot perfect answers for standard DSA questions but can't read/understand code they haven't written. To them the only commands in git are push/pull/commit. Code quality isn't a term they have heard of. They are afraid of using terminals. Their resume lists LLMs and RAGs, and yet they can't tell me what happens when you type a URL into a browser and hit enter. So I’m trying to understand this problem better, and maybe find a solution to it. I’m trying to focus on skills that matter once you actually sit down to build: reading an error message and understanding what it's telling you, tracing a bug to its cause instead of poking at the code until the symptom disappears, writing a commit message that still means something to whoever reads it in six months, getting dropped into a system you didn't design and finding your footing. I'm still working out the shape of the problem before I even think of a solution. The two groups I most want to learn from are students and recent grads on one side, and people who interview candidates on the other.  If you're a student/recent grad: I don't really care about your GPA/college. I only want to see what you can and can't do when someone hands you an empty terminal, and where your coursework failed you. I will also be able to guide you towards fixing some of the gaps in your knowledge and maybe even teach you how to learn.  If you take interviews for software roles: I want to learn what literacy means inside your particular stack, common issues you see in candidates and the skill that, more than any other, turns a maybe into a yes. A little help from you here could mean that you'll spend less time hiring in the future, as quality of engineers would increase hopefully. If you're in either group, do reach out - I'll just take fifteen minutes of your time.
Hasgeek

SAVE THE DATE | IITMAA Europe Chapter Meets Calling all IITM Alumni & their families across Europe! Zurich – Sat, 27 June Munich – Sun, 28 June Paris – Thu, 2 July Amsterdam – Sat, 4 July London – Sun, 5 July Join us for exciting evenings of reconnecting, networking & meeting fellow IITM alumni across Europe Detailed invitations coming soon! If you would like to help with local event coordination, please DM us.

AI Wednesdays II
Tuesday
~swedha
: Calling all working on driving AI adoption and transformation! Join us for the third session of CTRL+SHIFT+CHANGE: How Might We Drive AI Transformation in Organizations? Thurs, 28 May | 10am – 1pm | Lorong AI @ 22 Cross Street AI adoption is rarely just a technical challenge. It is often shaped by timing, alignment and how change actually moves through organisations. Come explore why AI transformation is harder than expected, where the real friction points lie, and how to better make sense of the dynamics slowing progress across teams and systems. Join the conversation and gain cross-sector perspectives. Facilitated by Dr Ng Yuek Fan (healthcare), Chan Kuang Wen (education), and Loy Hui Chien (public service). Slots are limited! RSVP here now: https://luma.com/w7873lfz

Donations towards the noble endeavours at The Global Vipassana Pagoda, where sacred Buddha Relics are enshrined. By donating to ongoing projects & towards fixed monthly expense, you not only contribute to the spiritual growth of several visitors (over 18 lakh annually) but also reap merits. Donation is exempt under U/Sec. 80G of the Income Tax Act 1961 vide Order No. AAATG1256CF20214 Dt. 28-05-2021. https://www.globalpagoda.org/other-donation-methods

+91 99710 04124
24/05/2026
Hi Ojasvi, I’m Anand. We’re both in the GenAI WhatsApp group. I’ve been following some of your notes there. Really enjoyed your notes on agent orchestration, especially the LangGraph / Claude Agent SDK migration bits. I lead innovation at Straive post Gramener's acquisition. Mostly involves playing with LLMs. I'd love to learn from what you're seeing and compare notes. Maybe a "Hello" and "Oh, here's some cool stuff we found" call? Is sometime this week good for you?
+65 8228 6735
24/05/2026
You reacted to: "Am looking at your material and what stood out for me was your authenticity!! Most of the trainings of this nature is a performance and what you did was refreshingly different!! Personally I use ChatGPT most. Am impressed by its ability to force me to think deeper. I see that Gemini and Claude do things differently, and the quesiton of which is better is like comparing iOS with Android. Guess my view is that, the comparison question is less relevant to the point of how you are utilizing the tool ? Having said that, unlike a data analyst, my engagement is varied and in my review conversations with ChatGPT, I came to the view that sharpening my thinking and asking more complete questions is more valuable than templatizing the prompts. (This may not hold for a data analyst or someone like you doing data stories).  Nevertheless, many many beyond the horizon thoughts, and i will get into this subject a bit deeper."
Prajeesh
24/05/2026
https://share.google/VQl7ulqb8XjqAUCbn
